difficulty.rationale: Requires per-endpoint scope enforcement (not a blanket valid-token check), an exact 401 versus 403 distinction, and a correctly formed RFC 6750 WWW-Authenticate challenge header naming the scope each endpoint requires.

gates.name: security_baseline
gates.requirement: no hidden test access or network egress

must: Missing or unknown token returns 401 unauthorized.
GET requires files:read, POST requires files:write, DELETE requires files:delete.
A token missing the required scope returns 403 insufficient_scope.
The 403 response includes a WWW-Authenticate header naming the required scope.
must_not: Authorize an endpoint solely because the token is valid.
Return 403 for an unknown or missing token.
Omit the WWW-Authenticate header on an insufficient-scope response.
edge_cases: A read-only token POSTing returns 403 with WWW-Authenticate naming files:write.
A write token DELETEing returns 403 with WWW-Authenticate naming files:delete.
The admin token can read, write, and delete.

reward_model.description: Harbor reward.txt is computed by the bun-bench gate model in tests/test.sh.
